ddls:
CREATE TABLE "patches" (
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patch */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the patch */,
  "patch_file" TEXT /* File name of the patch */,
  "base_kernel_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the base kernel version */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the status of the patch */,
  "patch_home_page" TEXT /* Link to the patch home page */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments about the patch */,
  "patch_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the patch */,
  "patch_size" INTEGER /* Size of the patch file in bytes */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the patch was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the patch was last updated */,
  "submitted_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the patch */,
  "reviewed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reviewed the patch */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the patch */,
  "merged_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the patch was merged into the mainline */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patches_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES statuses ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patches_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patches_reviewed_by FOREIGN KEY ("reviewed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patches_submitted_by FOREIGN KEY ("submitted_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patches_base_kernel_id FOREIGN KEY ("base_kernel_id") REFERENCES kernels ("kernel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "kernels" (
  "kernel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each kernel version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the kernel */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the kernel version */,
  "released_at" TEXT /* Date when the kernel version was released */,
  "is_stable" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the kernel version is considered stable */,
  "maintainer" INTEGER /* ID of the user who maintains the kernel version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("kernel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_kernels_maintainer FOREIGN KEY ("maintainer") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., experimental, tested, mainlined) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  "is_final" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the status is a final state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "platforms" (
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each platform */,
  "platform_name" TEXT /* Name of the platform (e.g., PPC, MIPS) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the platform */,
  "architecture" TEXT /* Specific architecture of the platform (e.g., 32-bit, 64-bit) */,
  "is_supported" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the platform is supported by the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patch_platforms" (
  "patch_platform_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patch-platform relationship */,
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patch */,
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* ID of the platform */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patch_platform_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patch_platforms_platform_id FOREIGN KEY ("platform_id") REFERENCES platforms ("platform_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patch_platforms_patch_id FOREIGN KEY ("patch_id") REFERENCES patches ("patch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, tester, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the user has admin privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patch being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the patch */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the patch was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the patch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_patch_id FOREIGN KEY ("patch_id") REFERENCES patches ("patch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patch being tested */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who conducted the test */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test was conducted */,
  "test_result" TEXT /* Result of the test (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments about the test */,
  "test_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the test in seconds */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test was conducted (e.g., development, staging, production) */,
  "test_configuration" TEXT /* Configuration settings used during the test */,
  "test_output" TEXT /* Output of the test (e.g., log files, error messages) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_patch_id FOREIGN KEY ("patch_id") REFERENCES patches ("patch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patch being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the comment was made */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "parent_comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment (for threading) */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the comment is public or private */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the comment has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_parent_comment_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_comment_id") REFERENCES comments ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_patch_id FOREIGN KEY ("patch_id") REFERENCES patches ("patch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patch */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the patch */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the version was created */,
  "changelog" TEXT /* Changelog for the version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the version was last updated */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the version is the current version */,
  "is_stable" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the version is considered stable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_patch_id FOREIGN KEY ("patch_id") REFERENCES patches ("patch_id")
)
